steps.title: Solve for x efficiently
steps.content:
Start with the given equation: $$4x + 7 = 15$$. Subtract 7 from both sides: $$4x = 8$$. Divide by 4: $$x = 2$$.

Now compute the target expression $$8x - 3$$ by substituting $$x = 2$$: $$8(2) - 3 = 16 - 3 = 13$$.

Alternatively, avoid solving for x: Double the equation (to get $$8x + 14 = 30$$), then subtract 17 from both sides to reach $$8x - 3 = 13$$.

steps.title: Final answer confirmation
steps.content: Either method confirms the value of $$8x - 3$$ is 13. That matches choice B — B is correct.
